DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Micah Jacobs, Esq. (415-445-4696) of Jacobs Law Group SF for Plaintiff/ opposing party LOYTR.
Crytsal Gaudette, Esq. (831-373-7500) for Defendant/Moving party TINYCO, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the basis for the tentative ruling. Defendant argues two points. Plaintiff argues damages. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with addition as follows:

DEFENDANT TINYCO, INC.'s DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF LOYTR INC'S UNVERIFIED 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendant TinyCo, Inc.'s demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to fourth cause of action for breach of contract and the sixth cause of action for unfair business practices and overruled as to the fifth cause of action for fraud, concealment and misrepresentation. Plaintiff Loytr, Inc. has not alleged, nor does it appear reasonably possible that it could allege, that it suffered any cognizable contract damages resulting from TinyCo's alleged violation of COPPA, which is an essential element of the fourth cause of action. Similarly, Loytr does not allege, nor does it appear reasonably possible that it could allege, that it suffered an injury in fact and has lost money or property resulting from TinyCo's alleged violation of COPPA, which is an essential element of the sixth cause of action. Loyter has adequately alleged sufficient facts to support a claim for promissory fraud, which requires that its fifth cause of action be permitted to go forward beyond the pleading stage.

This ruling is without prejudice to Loytr's ability to seek leave from the trial judge to to bring the COPPA claims back in the case. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)
Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Micah R. Jacobs, of Jacobs Law Group SF, Tel. 415.445.4696, for plaintiffs;
Asha Rita Subas, of Kennedy, Archer & Giffin, Tel. 831.373.7500, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court modifies its tentative ruling to permit new subclaims for breach of contract and accounting claims. PLAINTIFF LOYTR, INC., Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint is denied in large part. Loytr is granted leave to file a first amended complaint to add additional factual allegations in support of its existing claims, but is not permitted to add Jam City as a defendant. While there is a policy favoring the liberal allowance of amendments to pleadings, that policy does not apply where, as here, a new defendant and several new claims are sought to be added three and a half months before trial. Loytr fails to persuasively explain why it did not seek to add Jam City or its proposed new claims many months ago. Adding them now greatly expands this case, and would either impair TinyCo and Jam City's ability to defend this case including precluding either of them from bringing a summary judgment motion or require that the trial date be continued for many months. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Micah R. Jacobs, of Jacobs Law Group SF, Tel. 415.445.4696, for plaintiffs;
Phil K. Rucker, of Kennedy, Archer & Giffen, Tel. 831.373.7500, for defendants & x-complainants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT LOYTR, INC., DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT as to all seven causes of action filed by cross-complainant TinyCo, Inc. is sustained without leave to amend as to all seven causes of action. Loytr filed this case on April 15, 2016. The limitations period for TinyCo's common law fraud claims is three years and the limitations period for TinyCo's Unfair Competition Law, rescission and breach of written contract claims is four years. TinyCo's common law fraud claims are based on the allegations that Loytr concealed facts and made misrepresentations regarding the number of Loytr's users and Loytr's willingness and ability to promote TinyCo's games. Paragraph 88 of the FACC states that TinyCo's knew about Loyter's alleged fraud no later than May 2, 2012, which renders the first through third causes of action time-barred. (See also FACC paragraphs 27 and 36). TinyCo's UCL, rescission and breach of contract claims are all based in whole or substantial part on the allegation that Loytr misrepresented its willingness to promote and/or refused to promote TinyCo's games. (FACC paragraphs 80, 86, 94 and 100). The third bullet point in paragraph 27 of the FACC states that TinyCo knew about Loytr's refusal to promote TinyCo's games no later than February 27, 2012, which renders the fourth through seventh causes time-barred. TinyCo has not alleged nor is it reasonably possible for it to allege that the delayed discovery, equitable tolling and/or equitable estoppel doctrines apply to the facts alleged in the FACC. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF LOYTR, INC.'S Motion For Protective Order Staying Discovery On First Amended Cross-Complaint and placing limits on the deposition of Cole Ratias is DENIED in its entirety. Defendant TinyCo, Inc.'s request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Loytr's failure to file its demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint prior to seeking a stay of discovery as to that pleading is alone sufficient grounds to deny the stay. Moreover, Loytr fails to show that it would suffer any undue burden by permitting the discovery it seeks to have stayed go forward as currently scheduled. TinyCo's request for $10,000 in sanctions is wholly unsupported and appears to seek an unwarranted windfall instead of the shifting of expenses incurred due to conduct that lacked substantial justification. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
